message.content:
Read these specific files in the Hyperswitch codebase at /Users/hemanth-maddi/Documents/hyperswitch/hyperswitch/ and return the full content (or key sections) of each:

1. `/crates/api_models/src/routing.rs` - Read lines 1-150 (the routing algorithm types and StraightThroughAlgorithm enum)
2. `/crates/router/src/core/payments/routing.rs` - Read lines 1-100 and then search for the function `perform_straight_through_routing` and show its full body
3. `/crates/router/src/core/routing.rs` - Read lines 1-80
4. Show the structure of `/crates/router/src/core/payments/` directory (all files)
5. Read `/config/config.example.toml` - first 100 lines (to see how config is structured)
6. Read `/docker-compose.yml` - full file
7. Check if there's a dummy/test connector - search for "dummy_connector" or "test_connector" in the crates directory (just names/paths, not content)
8. Read `/crates/router/src/routes.rs` or equivalent routing setup file - first 50 lines to understand how API routes are registered

I need this to understand exactly where to hook in a new BIN-based routing algorithm. Focus on reading the exact code, not summarizing.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll help you explore the Hyperswitch codebase to understand the routing architecture. Let me start by reading these files systematically.

1	# This is a sample config file whose sole purpose is to enumerate
2	# all the available configuration options, and is intended to be used
3	# solely as a reference. Please copy this file to create a config.
4	
5	# This is used to identify the running application instance
6	# Check common_enums::ApplicationSource for available options
7	# For more details - https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/issues/10489
8	application_source = "main"
9	
10	# Server configuration
11	[server]
12	port = 8080
13	host = "127.0.0.1"
14	workers = 10                                 # Number of worker threads to handle requests (defaults to physical CPU count)
15	# This is the grace time (in seconds) given to the actix-server to stop the execution
16	# For more details: https://actix.rs/docs/server/#graceful-shutdown
17	shutdown_timeout = 30
18	# HTTP Request body limit. Defaults to 32kB
19	request_body_limit = 32_768
20	keep_alive = 5                                # Keep-alive timeout in seconds
21	client_request_timeout = 5000                 # Client request timeout in milliseconds
22	client_disconnect_timeout = 1000              # Client disconnect timeout in milliseconds
23	
24	# HTTPS Server Configuration
25	# Self-signed Private Key and Certificate can be generated with mkcert for local development
26	[server.tls]
27	port = 8081
28	host = "127.0.0.1"
29	private_key = "/path/to/private_key.pem"
30	certificate = "/path/to/certificate.pem"
31	
32	# Proxy server configuration for connecting to payment gateways.
33	# Don't define the fields if a Proxy isn't needed. Empty strings will cause failure.
34	[proxy]
35	# http_url = "http proxy url"                   # Proxy all HTTP traffic via this proxy
36	# https_url = "https proxy url"                 # Proxy all HTTPS traffic via this proxy
37	idle_pool_connection_timeout = 90               # Timeout for idle pool connections (defaults to 90s)
38	bypass_proxy_hosts = "localhost, cluster.local" # A comma-separated list of domains or IP addresses that should not use the proxy. Whitespace between entries would be ignored.
39	
40	# Configuration for the Key Manager Service
41	[key_manager]
42	url = "http://localhost:5000" # URL of the encryption service
43	# Enable / disable legacy key store decryption behavior.
44	# Set to true if upgrading from previous versions of Hyperswitch.
45	# Set to false for new deployments to use newer / correct decryption behavior.
46	use_legacy_key_store_decryption = false
47	
48	# Main SQL data store credentials
49	[master_database]
50	username = "db_user"      # DB Username
51	password = "db_pass"      # DB Password. Use base-64 encoded kms encrypted value here when kms is enabled
52	host = "localhost"        # DB Host
53	port = 5432               # DB Port
54	dbname = "hyperswitch_db" # Name of Database
55	pool_size = 5             # Number of connections to keep open
56	connection_timeout = 10   # Timeout for database connection in seconds
57	queue_strategy = "Fifo"   # Add the queue strategy used by the database bb8 client
58	
59	# Replica SQL data store credentials
60	[replica_database]
61	username = "replica_user" # DB Username
62	password = "db_pass"      # DB Password. Use base-64 encoded kms encrypted value here when kms is enabled
63	host = "localhost"        # DB Host
64	port = 5432               # DB Port
65	dbname = "hyperswitch_db" # Name of Database
66	pool_size = 5             # Number of connections to keep open
67	connection_timeout = 10   # Timeout for database connection in seconds
68	queue_strategy = "Fifo"   # Add the queue strategy used by the database bb8 client
69	
70	# Redis credentials
71	[redis]
72	host = "127.0.0.1"
73	port = 6379
74	pool_size = 5                     # Number of connections to keep open
75	reconnect_max_attempts = 5        # Maximum number of reconnection attempts to make before failing. Set to 0 to retry forever.
76	reconnect_delay = 5               # Delay between reconnection attempts, in milliseconds
77	default_ttl = 300                 # Default TTL for entries, in seconds
78	default_hash_ttl = 900            # Default TTL for hashes entries, in seconds
79	use_legacy_version = false        # Resp protocol for fred crate (set this to true if using RESPv2 or redis version < 6)
80	stream_read_count = 1             # Default number of entries to read from stream if not provided in stream read options
81	auto_pipeline = true              # Whether or not the client should automatically pipeline commands across tasks when possible.
82	broadcast_channel_capacity = 32   # The default capacity used when creating tokio broadcast channels
83	disable_auto_backpressure = false # Whether or not to disable the automatic backpressure features when pipelining is enabled.
84	max_in_flight_commands = 5000     # The maximum number of in-flight commands (per connection) before backpressure will be applied.
85	default_command_timeout = 30      # An optional timeout to apply to all commands. In seconds
86	unresponsive_timeout = 10         # An optional timeout for Unresponsive commands in seconds. This should be less than default_command_timeout.
87	unresponsive_check_interval = 2   # The frequency at which the client checks for unresponsive connections. In seconds. This value should usually be less than half of `unresponsive_timeout` and always more than 1 ms.
88	max_failure_threshold_seconds = 5         # Max seconds Redis can be unreachable before server shutdown (min 5s)
89	max_feed_count = 200              # The maximum number of frames that will be fed to a socket before flushing.
90	
91	# This section provides configs for currency conversion api
92	[forex_api]
93	api_key = ""                      # Api key for making request to foreign exchange Api
94	fallback_api_key = ""             # Api key for the fallback service
95	redis_ttl_in_seconds = 172800     # Time to expire for forex data stored in Redis
96	data_expiration_delay_in_seconds = 21600   # Expiration time for data in cache as well as redis in seconds
97	redis_lock_timeout_in_seconds = 100        # Redis remains write locked for 100 s once the acquire_redis_lock is called
98	
99	# Logging configuration. Logging can be either to file or console or both.
100

1	volumes:
2	  pg_data:
3	  redisinsight_store:
4	  ckh_data:
5	
6	networks:
7	  router_net:
8	
9	services:
10	  ### Dependencies
11	  prestart-hook:
12	    image: docker.io/curlimages/curl-base:latest
13	    container_name: prestart-hook
14	    environment:
15	      - ONE_CLICK_SETUP=${ONE_CLICK_SETUP:-false}
16	    entrypoint:
17	      [
18	        "/bin/sh",
19	        "-c",
20	        "apk add --no-cache bash && /bin/bash /prestart_hook.sh",
21	      ]
22	    volumes:
23	      - ./scripts/prestart_hook.sh:/prestart_hook.sh
24	    networks:
25	      - router_net
26	
27	  pg:
28	    image: docker.io/postgres:latest
29	    ports:
30	      - "5432:5432"
31	    networks:
32	      - router_net
33	    volumes:
34	      - pg_data:/var/lib/postgresql
35	    environment:
36	      - POSTGRES_USER=db_user
37	      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=db_pass
38	      - POSTGRES_DB=hyperswitch_db
39	    healthcheck:
40	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -d $${POSTGRES_DB} -U $${POSTGRES_USER}"]
41	      interval: 5s
42	      retries: 3
43	      start_period: 5s
44	      timeout: 5s
45	
46	  redis-standalone:
47	    image: docker.io/redis:7
48	    networks:
49	      - router_net
50	    ports:
51	      - "6379:6379"
52	    healthcheck:
53	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "redis-cli ping | grep '^PONG$'"]
54	      interval: 5s
55	      retries: 3
56	      start_period: 5s
57	      timeout: 5s
58	
59	  migration_runner:
60	    image: docker.io/debian:trixie-slim
61	    pull_policy: always
62	    command: >
63	      bash -c "
64	      apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl xz-utils &&
65	      curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -LsSf https://github.com/diesel-rs/diesel/releases/download/v2.3.5/diesel_cli-installer.sh | bash &&
66	      if ! command -v just >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://just.systems/install.sh | bash -s -- --to /usr/local/bin
68	      fi &&
69	      export PATH="$${PATH}:$${HOME}/.cargo/bin" &&
70	      just migrate"
71	    working_dir: /app
72	    networks:
73	      - router_net
74	    volumes:
75	      - ./:/app
76	    environment:
77	      # format -> postgresql://DB_USER:DB_PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DATABASE_NAME
78	      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://db_user:db_pass@pg:5432/hyperswitch_db
79	
80	  superposition:
81	    image: ghcr.io/juspay/superposition-demo:0.102.0
82	    ports:
83	      - "8081:8080"
84	    networks:
85	      - router_net
86	    environment:
87	      - API_HOSTNAME=http://localhost:8081
88	      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis-standalone:6379
89	    depends_on:
90	      redis-standalone:
91	        condition: service_healthy
92	
93	  superposition-init:
94	    image: alpine:latest
95	    depends_on:
96	      superposition:
97	        condition: service_started
98	    networks:
99	      - router_net
100	    volumes:
101	      - ./config/superposition_seed.toml:/seed.toml:ro
102	      - ./scripts/seed_superposition.sh:/seed_superposition.sh:ro
103	    environment:
104	      - SUPERPOSITION_URL=http://superposition:8080
105	      - SEED_FILE=/seed.toml
106	      - WORKSPACE_ID=dev
107	      - ORG_ID=localorg
108	    command: sh -c "apk add --no-cache curl jq yq && sh /seed_superposition.sh"
109	    restart: "no"
110	
111	  mailhog:
112	    image: docker.io/mailhog/mailhog
113	    networks:
114	      - router_net
115	    profiles:
116	      - full_setup
117	    ports:
118	      - "1025:1025"
119	      - "8025:8025"
120	
121	  ### Application services
122	  hyperswitch-server:
123	    image: docker.juspay.io/juspaydotin/hyperswitch-router:standalone
124	    pull_policy: always
125	    command: /local/bin/router -f /local/config/docker_compose.toml
126	    ports:
127	      - "8080:8080"
128	    networks:
129	      - router_net
130	    volumes:
131	      - ./config:/local/config
132	      - ./files:/local/bin/files
133	    depends_on:
134	      pg:
135	        condition: service_healthy
136	      redis-standalone:
137	        condition: service_healthy
138	      migration_runner:
139	        condition: service_completed_successfully
140	      superposition-init:
141	        condition: service_completed_successfully
142	    labels:
143	      logs: "promtail"
144	    healthcheck:
145	      test: curl --fail http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1
146	      interval: 5s
147	      retries: 3
148	      start_period: 5s
149	      timeout: 5s
150	
151	  hyperswitch-producer:
152	    image: docker.juspay.io/juspaydotin/hyperswitch-producer:standalone
153	    pull_policy: always
154	    command: /local/bin/scheduler -f /local/config/docker_compose.toml
155	    networks:
156	      - router_net
157	    profiles:
158	      - scheduler
159	    volumes:
160	      - ./config:/local/config
161	    environment:
162	      - SCHEDULER_FLOW=producer
163	    depends_on:
164	      hyperswitch-consumer:
165	        condition: service_healthy
166	    labels:
167	      logs: "promtail"
168	
169	  hyperswitch-consumer:
170	    image: docker.juspay.io/juspaydotin/hyperswitch-consumer:standalone
171	    pull_policy: always
172	    command: /local/bin/scheduler -f /local/config/docker_compose.toml
173	    networks:
174	      - router_net
175	    profiles:
176	      - scheduler
177	    volumes:
178	      - ./config:/local/config
179	    environment:
180	      - SCHEDULER_FLOW=consumer
181	    depends_on:
182	      hyperswitch-server:
183	        condition: service_healthy
184	    labels:
185	      logs: "promtail"
186	    healthcheck:
187	      test: (ps -e | grep scheduler) || exit 1
188	      interval: 10s
189	      retries: 3
190	      start_period: 5s
191	      timeout: 10s
192	
193	  hyperswitch-drainer:
194	    image: docker.juspay.io/juspaydotin/hyperswitch-drainer:standalone
195	    pull_policy: always
196	    command: /local/bin/drainer -f /local/config/docker_compose.toml
197	    deploy:
198	      replicas: ${DRAINER_INSTANCE_COUNT:-1}
199	    networks:
200	      - router_net
201	    profiles:
202	      - full_kv
203	    volumes:
204	      - ./config:/local/config
205	    restart: unless-stopped
206	    depends_on:
207	      hyperswitch-server:
208	        condition: service_healthy
209	    labels:
210	      logs: "promtail"
211	
212	  ### Web Client
213	  hyperswitch-web:
214	    image: docker.juspay.io/juspaydotin/hyperswitch-web:latest
215	    pull_policy: always
216	    ports:
217	      - "9050:9050"
218	    networks:
219	      - router_net
220	    depends_on:
221	      hyperswitch-server:
222	        condition: service_healthy
223	    environment:
224	      - ENABLE_LOGGING=true
225	      - SDK_ENV=local
226	      - ENV_LOGGING_URL=http://localhost:3103
227	      - ENV_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8080
228	    labels:
229	      logs: "promtail"
230	
231	  ### Control Center
232	  hyperswitch-control-center:
233	    image: docker.juspay.io/juspaydotin/hyperswitch-control-center:latest
234	    pull_policy: always
235	    ports:
236	      - "9000:9000"
237	    environment:
238	      - configPath=/tmp/dashboard-config.toml
239	      - themeConfigPath=/tmp/dashboard-theme.json
240	    volumes:
241	      - ./config/dashboard.toml:/tmp/dashboard-config.toml
242	      - ./config/dashboard_theme.json:/tmp/dashboard-theme.json
243	    depends_on:
244	      hyperswitch-server:
245	        condition: service_healthy
246	      hyperswitch-web:
247	        condition: service_started
248	    labels:
249	      logs: "promtail"
250	
251	  create-default-user:
252	    image: docker.io/curlimages/curl-base:latest
253	    container_name: create-default-user
254	    depends_on:
255	      hyperswitch-server:
256	        condition: service_healthy
257	      hyperswitch-control-center:
258	        condition: service_started
259	    environment:
260	      - HYPERSWITCH_SERVER_URL=http://hyperswitch-server:8080
261	      - HYPERSWITCH_CONTROL_CENTER_URL=http://hyperswitch-control-center:9000
262	    entrypoint:
263	      [
264	        "/bin/sh",
265	        "-c",
266	        "apk add --no-cache bash jq && /bin/bash /create_default_user.sh",
267	      ]
268	    volumes:
269	      - ./scripts/create_default_user.sh:/create_default_user.sh
270	    networks:
271	      - router_net
272	
273	  poststart-hook:
274	    image: docker.io/curlimages/curl-base:latest
275	    container_name: poststart-hook
276	    depends_on:
277	      hyperswitch-server:
278	        condition: service_healthy # Ensures it only starts when `hyperswitch-server` is healthy
279	    environment:
280	      - ONE_CLICK_SETUP=${ONE_CLICK_SETUP:-false}
281	    entrypoint:
282	      [
283	        "/bin/sh",
284	        "-c",
285	        "apk add --no-cache bash jq && /bin/bash /poststart_hook.sh",
286	      ]
287	    volumes:
288	      - ./scripts/poststart_hook.sh:/poststart_hook.sh
289	    networks:
290	      - router_net
291	
292	  ### Clustered Redis setup
293	  redis-cluster:
294	    image: docker.io/redis:7
295	    deploy:
296	      replicas: ${REDIS_CLUSTER_COUNT:-3}
297	    command: redis-server /usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf
298	    profiles:
299	      - clustered_redis
300	    volumes:
301	      - ./config/redis.conf:/usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf
302	    networks:
303	      - router_net
304	    ports:
305	      - "6379"
306	      - "16379"
307	
308	  redis-init:
309	    image: docker.io/redis:7
310	    profiles:
311	      - clustered_redis
312	    depends_on:
313	      - redis-cluster
314	    networks:
315	      - router_net
316	    command: |-
317	      bash -c 'export COUNT=${REDIS_CLUSTER_COUNT:-3}
318	      if [ $$COUNT -lt 3 ]
319	      then
320	      echo \"Minimum 3 nodes are needed for redis cluster\"
321	      exit 1
322	      fi
323	      HOSTS=\"\"
324	      for ((c=1; c<=$$COUNT;c++))
325	      do
326	      NODE=$COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME-redis-cluster-$$c:6379
327	      echo $$NODE
328	      HOSTS=\"$$HOSTS $$NODE\"
329	      done
330	      echo Creating a cluster with $$HOSTS
331	      redis-cli --cluster create $$HOSTS --cluster-yes
332	      '
333	  ### Monitoring
334	  grafana:
335	    image: docker.io/grafana/grafana:latest
336	    ports:
337	      - "3000:3000"
338	    networks:
339	      - router_net
340	    profiles:
341	      - monitoring
342	    restart: unless-stopped
343	    environment:

640	impl StaticRoutingAlgorithm {
641	    pub fn should_validate_connectors_in_routing_config(&self) -> bool {
642	        match self {
643	            Self::Single(_) | Self::Priority(_) | Self::VolumeSplit(_) | Self::Advanced(_) => true,
644	            Self::ThreeDsDecisionRule(_) => false,
645	        }
646	    }
647	}
648	
649	#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize, ToSchema)]
650	#[serde(tag = "type", content = "data", rename_all = "snake_case")]
651	pub enum RoutingAlgorithmSerde {
652	    Single(Box<RoutableConnectorChoice>),
653	    Priority(Vec<RoutableConnectorChoice>),
654	    VolumeSplit(Vec<ConnectorVolumeSplit>),
655	    Advanced(Program<ConnectorSelection>),
656	    ThreeDsDecisionRule(Program<ThreeDSDecisionRule>),
657	}
658	
659	impl TryFrom<RoutingAlgorithmSerde> for StaticRoutingAlgorithm {
660	    type Error = error_stack::Report<ParsingError>;
661	
662	    fn try_from(value: RoutingAlgorithmSerde) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
663	        match &value {
664	            RoutingAlgorithmSerde::Priority(i) if i.is_empty() => {
665	                Err(ParsingError::StructParseFailure(
666	                    "Connectors list can't be empty for Priority Algorithm",
667	                ))?
668	            }
669	            RoutingAlgorithmSerde::VolumeSplit(i) if i.is_empty() => {
670	                Err(ParsingError::StructParseFailure(
671	                    "Connectors list can't be empty for Volume split Algorithm",
672	                ))?
673	            }
674	            _ => {}
675	        };
676	        Ok(match value {
677	            RoutingAlgorithmSerde::Single(i) => Self::Single(i),
678	            RoutingAlgorithmSerde::Priority(i) => Self::Priority(i),
679	            RoutingAlgorithmSerde::VolumeSplit(i) => Self::VolumeSplit(i),
680	            RoutingAlgorithmSerde::Advanced(i) => Self::Advanced(i),
681	            RoutingAlgorithmSerde::ThreeDsDecisionRule(i) => Self::ThreeDsDecisionRule(i),
682	        })
683	    }
684	}

312	                }
